subject: Public Health
title: Herd Immunity
description: A micro-lesson that makes Herd Immunity usable.

hook.text: When enough people are immune, even the unvaccinated are protected. That's herd immunity.

buildup.text: A virus can't spread if it keeps landing on immune hosts—the chain of transmission breaks.

discovery.text: For measles the threshold is about 95%—almost everyone must be vaccinated to protect the rest.

twist.text: Herd immunity protects newborns and immunocompromised people who can't be vaccinated themselves.

climax.text: When vaccination rates drop below the threshold, outbreaks return—as measles proved in 2019.

punchline.text: Your immunity protects your neighbor's baby.

quiz.question: What is the herd immunity threshold for measles?
